0.description: ISO language code (e.g. en, fr, de)

1.description: A reference to the specific item in the vocabulary referenced by cdi:vocabulary, using a URI or other resolvable identifier.
The value of the entry of the controlled vocabulary. If no controlled vocabulary is used the term is entered here and none of the properties defining the controlled vocabulary location are used.
The name of the code list (controlled vocabulary).
If the value of the string is "Other" or the equivalent from the codelist, this attribute can provide a more specific value not found in the codelist.
A reference to the external controlled vocabulary, using a URI or other resolvable identifier.

Description of the value domain constraints (0..1). Inline ValueAndConceptDescription or @id reference.
Enumeration domain providing the set of permissible coded values (0..1). Inline EnumerationDomain or @id reference.
Reference to a conceptual domain (SubstantiveConceptualDomain or SentinelConceptualDomain) that provides conceptual grounding for this value domain (0..1). @id reference only.

(SentinelValueDomain only) The type of software platform under which sentinel codes will be used. Statistical software platforms use different sets of codes to indicate missing values. Example values: SASNumeric, SPSSstyle, Rstyle, StataNumeric, BlankString, EmptyString, Unrestricted.
